criterion: diameter contracts from 2.207 to < eps=0.05; time-to-eps grows ~ log(1/eps) (positive slope)
explanation: We integrate the characteristic flow x' = P_x^perp A_B[mu](x) and record the cloud diameter over time (panel A, log scale -- a straight line is exponential decay). Panel B plots the first time the diameter drops below eps against log(1/eps) for a geometric sweep of eps; a positive-slope linear fit confirms the O(log 1/eps) rate.
hypothesis: Self-attention with B = beta I on a measure supported in an open hemisphere contracts its geodesic convex hull to a single point (Proposition 2.1); the diameter decays exponentially, so the time to reach diameter eps grows like O(log(1/eps)).
